scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: covers the account/user/service metadata surface practical for reverse-ETL reads (services, current_user, current_customer, datacenters, per-service details fan-out, per-customer users fan-out). Fastly is read-only for reverse-ETL purposes -- legacy's own documented rationale ("the Fastly API has no obvious safe reverse-ETL write surface") still holds after full-surface research: every mutation endpoint below either provisions/destroys billable infrastructure (services, versions, domains, backends, TLS certs, WAF/ACL/dictionary config) or manages account security (users, tokens, roles), none of which is a safe declarative reverse-ETL write target. The full documented API spans ~20 resource collections (services, TLS, purging, ACLs/dictionaries, load balancing, Next-Gen WAF, observability, logging endpoints for 20+ third-party log destinations, billing, IAM); this manifest enumerates every collection's representative endpoints and closes each out with a specific category+reason rather than a blanket bucket.

excluded.reason: provisions a new billable service; not a reverse-ETL read/write target
mutates service identity/configuration metadata; legacy never wrote to Fastly
irreversibly deletes a service; no safe reverse-ETL equivalent
look up one service by name; duplicates the services stream's per-record name field, no additional data
single-service summary is a strict subset of service_details' fuller per-service response
per-version domain config is VCL-service editing metadata, not account/service inventory; no legacy parity and no clean single-level fan_out (nests under service AND version)
creates a new draft config version; provisioning action, not a data read
version list is VCL config history; service_details already surfaces the active/current version summary
single version detail nests under service_id AND version_id, a 2-level fan-out the engine's single-level fan_out dialect cannot express
mutates a draft VCL config version
activates a config version to production; a live-traffic-affecting deploy action
deactivates a production config version; a live-traffic-affecting deploy action
clones a config version, creating new provisioned state
locks a config version; an administrative state change
validation check action, returns pass/fail diagnostics rather than a data record
single-user lookup is a strict subset of the users stream's per-customer listing
creates a new account user; account security management, not a reverse-ETL write target
mutates a user's account/role; account security management
irreversibly removes a user account
changes the authenticated user's own credential; never a bulk/reverse-ETL action
triggers a credential-reset email; security-sensitive account action
single-customer lookup by id duplicates current_customer's own-account response
mutates billing/account-owner metadata
irreversibly deletes a customer account
invoicing/billing data requires a distinct elevated billing-read scope beyond the read tokens this connector expects; not modeled by legacy
API token management is credential-adjacent; enumerating live tokens is a security-sensitive listing, not modeled by legacy
creates a new live API credential; never a reverse-ETL write target
revokes a live API credential
Next-Gen WAF/ACL configuration is edge-security config, not account/service inventory data, and nests under service+version (2-level fan-out unsupported)
provisions a new ACL; edge-security config mutation
edge-config dictionaries are VCL service configuration, not account/service inventory; 2-level fan-out unsupported
mutates live edge-config dictionary data served to production traffic
purges cached content from production edge nodes; an operational action with no data-record shape, not a reverse-ETL write
purges an entire service's cache; production-affecting operational action
TLS certificate provisioning state is security/credential-adjacent infrastructure, out of this connector's read-only account-metadata scope
provisions a new TLS certificate subscription
legacy (classic) WAF configuration management, not account/service inventory data
Next-Gen WAF is a separate product surface (workspaces/rules/signals) with its own auth model; not modeled by legacy
load-balancing director/pool configuration is edge-routing config, not account/service metadata
real-time metrics/stats time-series endpoint, a monitoring query rather than a catalog resource with a stable record shape
Observability alerting configuration is a distinct product surface from account/service inventory

20+ real-time-logging third-party integration endpoints (S3, Splunk, Datadog, BigQuery, etc.), each its own resource family with provider-specific auth fields; VCL service logging config, not account/service inventory
API Security product is a separate add-on surface with its own scanning/campaign model, not modeled by legacy
DDoS Protection events are a separate security-product surface, not modeled by legacy
the newer Domain Management product surface manages DNS delegation, distinct from the legacy per-service domain config already excluded above
Client-Side Protection is a separate security-product configuration surface, not modeled by legacy
product-enablement status per service, an account-configuration flag set rather than a data catalog resource
an internal diagnostic utility endpoint, not a data resource
